propositions: Code review is too loose: zero 'BLOCK — finding' votes occurred across 162 post-recalibration changes because no real finding reached the 0.95 threshold, so a blocking threshold must be derived from adjudicated field data and enabled for serious-tier criteria in decide.py.

Agent guidance mandating remediation of ALL valid plan-review advisories (~13/round, ~95% of edits minting new findings) should be loosened to judgment so organic advisory uptake can be measured via norm_id survival across rounds.

Completion-verifier defects are structural: FAIL verdicts persist nowhere, FAILs ratchet piecemeal, and 'children closed (signed)' epic ACs defeat the certifiable:false escape hatch, so FAILs must be durably persisted with all unmet criteria in one verdict.

Leaf plans drift from their parent's declared scope with no plan-review criterion checking containment, so a new G7 criterion must gate leaf-vs-parent scope containment at the claim-gating review, shipped advisory per the expand-contract pattern.

b9c0's 0.54 code-review block threshold governs the code-v2 cohort only and is provisional, requiring a non-circular held-out adjudication before production reliance; code-v3 is a fresh calibration cohort out of scope for this epic.

Field observation on the plan-review claim gate showed margin verdict instability, INDETERMINATE grounding for not-yet-written new code, and one-per-round test-blast-radius enumeration, but the gate was high-precision and convergent once plans were made factually accurate against the source.
